steps.explanation: Apply the triangle inequality theorem for all three pairs of sides. Let the known sides be 5 and 9, and the unknown side be \(x\). The three conditions are: (1) \(5 + 9 > x\), (2) \(5 + x > 9\), and (3) \(9 + x > 5\).
Simplify each inequality. From (1): \(x < 14\). From (2): \(x > 4\). From (3): \(x > -4\), which is always true for any positive length.
Combine the binding constraints: \(x > 4\) and \(x < 14\), giving \(4 < x < 14\).

• Choice A: \(x < 4\) — allows only sides shorter than 4, but then \(5 + x < 9\) violates the inequality. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: \(x > 14\) — allows only sides longer than 14, but then \(5 + 9 = 14\) is not greater than \(x\). Eliminated.
  • Choice C: \(4 < x < 14\) — satisfies all three triangle inequality conditions simultaneously. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • Choice D: \(x < 4\) or \(x > 14\) — the union of the two eliminated ranges; neither region forms a valid triangle. Eliminated.
